items.description: ITEM-1: Add three new first-class artifact kinds — plan (PLAN-, folder plans), visual (VIS-, folder visuals), review (REV-, folder reviews) — to DEFAULT_KINDS/DEFAULT_BUCKETS in src/artifacts/registry.ts AND the repo-root wiki.json, kept byte-aligned so the loadStructure-matches-default check and registry-config.test.ts stay green. Each kind needs a templates/<kind>.md carrying a frontmatter schema (id, project, title, summary required; plus kind-appropriate fields) and authored body H2 sections. No blockers. Foundational for all other items.
steps: Add plan/visual/review to DEFAULT_KINDS with prefix, folder, dedup (true for plan/review, true for visual), and skill left unset for now (ITEM-4 wires skills).
Add matching entries to the repo-root wiki.json kinds map so it stays equal to DEFAULT_STRUCTURE; verify with the existing loadStructure-vs-DEFAULT_STRUCTURE comparison.

Create templates/plan.md, templates/visual.md, templates/review.md each with required id/project/title/summary fields and at least one authored H2 body section, following templates/doc.md as the shape reference.

Run a temp-vault smoke: wiki create plan --title <t> --summary <s> --project p --body - writes projects/p/plans/PLAN-0001-*.md; same for visual and review; wiki schema plan|visual|review prints the fields.
Update registry-config.test.ts (or add a test) to pin the three new kinds' prefix/folder, and confirm wiki create/schema for each works end-to-end in a temp vault.
Run all three gates green.

items.description: ITEM-2: Add a sidecar-asset write path to wiki create, routed through mintAndWrite in src/artifacts/store.ts (the one create/capture seam). A create flag (e.g. --asset <path>) adopts an existing external file (verbatim copy) into the vault beside the minted Markdown, with the same <ID>-<slug> basename and the source file's extension, and records the vault-relative asset path in a frontmatter asset field (reuse the existing file_ref field type). The Markdown spine stays the searchable/indexed artifact; the asset is copied as-is. Keep the copy outside the per-project lock (the lock wraps only allocate->write). Blocked by ITEM-1.
steps: Add an optional `asset` field (type file_ref) to the visual template (the primary asset-bearing kind); allow it on any kind generically.

Add a --asset <path> flag to wiki create that reads the source file, mints the artifact, then writes the asset beside the .md with basename <ID>-<slug> and the source extension; store the vault-relative path in the asset frontmatter field.
Route the sidecar write so it is part of the same create flow as mintAndWrite without holding the per-project lock during the file copy.
Fail clearly (non-zero, no partial artifact) when --asset points at a missing/unreadable file; do not leave an orphan .md or orphan asset.

Temp-vault end-to-end: wiki create visual --title <t> --summary <s> --project p --asset /tmp/x.html writes projects/p/visuals/VIS-0001-slug.md AND VIS-0001-slug.html with matching basenames, and the .md frontmatter asset field points at the .html; confirm wiki search (against a fake/mock qmd or the test's qmd harness) still finds the artifact by its Markdown text.
Add a test that fails if the asset basename diverges from the .md basename or the asset field is missing; run all three gates green.

items.description: ITEM-3: Asset retrieval by id. Extend wiki path (or add a focused verb) to resolve an artifact id to its sidecar asset path, including a --json result. Reading is a pure vault read (no qmd). An id with no asset returns a clear not-found-style result, not a crash. Blocked by ITEM-2.
steps: Resolve an artifact id to its asset path from the frontmatter asset field (verify the asset file exists on disk).
Surface it via wiki path <id> --asset (or an equivalent documented verb) printing the absolute asset path; --json returns { id, asset } (and { error } shape on stderr when absent).
An id that has no asset, or whose asset file is missing, returns a clear message and non-zero exit, never a stack trace.
Temp-vault end-to-end: create a visual with --asset, then resolve its asset path by id (text and --json), and confirm the path points at the on-disk .html.
Add a test for both the has-asset and no-asset cases; run all three gates green.

items.description: ITEM-4: Wire the skill->kind map and document the extension pattern. In the repo-root wiki.json (and DEFAULT_KINDS, kept aligned), set skill: improve on plan, skill: visual-explainer on visual, and a reviewer/review skill on review. Document in wiki.json's _comment and skills/wiki/SKILL.md how to add a new artifact-producing skill (add a kind with a skill field + a templates/<kind>.md; the per-runtime hook maps the skill to the kind). Blocked by ITEM-1.
steps: Set the skill field on plan (improve), visual (visual-explainer), and review (the review skill name) in both DEFAULT_KINDS and the repo-root wiki.json, kept byte-aligned with DEFAULT_STRUCTURE.

Confirm structure.skillForKind / the skill->kind reverse map resolves improve->plan, visual-explainer->visual, review-skill->review (mirror the existing skill-map assertions in registry-config.test.ts).

Update wiki.json _comment and the relevant SKILL.md section to describe the add-a-skill pattern (new kind + skill field + template) so future artifact-producing skills can be centralized without code changes.
Temp-vault check: the skill->kind map returns the new mappings; existing mappings (to-prd->prd, etc.) are unchanged.
Update/extend the skill-map test to pin the three new mappings; run all three gates green.

items.description: ITEM-5: Sidecar lifecycle integrity. The asset follows its artifact through delete, relocate, retitle, fmt, and doctor: wiki delete removes the asset alongside the .md; relocate/retitle move/rename the asset to keep the <ID>-<slug> basename paired with the .md; fmt's rename-to-id-slug renames the asset too; doctor does NOT flag a correctly-named sidecar asset as a loose/undeclared file. Blocked by ITEM-2.
steps: On wiki delete <id> [--force], remove the sidecar asset file in the same operation as the .md (and keep the existing inbound-link scrub behavior).
On retitle and cross/same-section relocate, move/rename the asset so its basename stays <new-ID-or-slug> matching the moved .md.
In fmt --write rename-to-id-slug, rename the asset alongside the .md so the pair never drifts.

In doctor's loose-file/structure check, treat a sidecar asset whose basename matches a sibling .md as part of that artifact (not a loose file); a truly orphaned asset (no matching .md) may still be reported.

Temp-vault end-to-end: create a visual with --asset, then (a) retitle it and confirm both files renamed and paired, (b) delete it and confirm both files gone, (c) run doctor and confirm the sidecar is not reported as drift; verify fmt --write keeps the pair aligned after an induced filename drift.
Add tests covering delete-removes-asset, retitle/relocate-moves-asset, and doctor-ignores-paired-sidecar; run all three gates green.
